vBRIEFInfo.description: Scope vBRIEF ingested from GitHub issue #1564

plan.title: task check: pyproject version freshness fails on master after v0.43.0

narratives.Description: The pyproject version freshness gate is red on master because local tag discovery expects v0.43.0 while pyproject.toml reports 0.44.0. This story makes the release/version freshness contract deterministic so task check is not red after a successful release sequence.
narratives.Overview:
## Summary

`task check` on current `origin/master` fails `tests/content/test_pyproject_version_freshness.py::test_pyproject_version_matches_latest_tag` because `pyproject.toml` reports `0.44.0` while the latest git tag is `v0.43.0`.

## Evidence

Observed while preparing the runtime-aware swarm guardrails PR. That PR does not modify `pyproject.toml` or the version freshness test; `git diff origin/master -- pyproject.toml tests/content/test_pyproject_version_freshness.py` is empty.

Failure excerpt:

```text
pyproject.toml [project].version drifted: got '0.44.0', expected '0.43.0' (PEP 440 normalization of latest tag 'v0.43.0')
```

## Expected follow-up

Decide whether `pyproject.toml` should be restored to the latest released tag version or whether the next release/tag flow should update the freshness expectation.
narratives.ImplementationPlan:
1. Inspect tests/content/test_pyproject_version_freshness.py, scripts/resolve_version.py, and pyproject.toml to determine whether the gate should follow the latest local tag, fetch/prune release tags, or treat post-release version bumps as intentional.
2. Add pytest verification evidence in tests/content/test_pyproject_version_freshness.py and any focused resolver tests needed so task check no longer fails on master after a normal release/tag flow.
narratives.UserStory: As a directive maintainer, I want the version freshness gate to match the actual release contract, so that task check is not red on master after a release.

narrative.Acceptance: Given pyproject.toml and release tags are present, when the freshness test runs, then it derives the expected version from the intended release source of truth rather than stale local tag state.

items.title: Master task check is green for version freshness

narrative.Acceptance: Given current master after the v0.44.0 release, when tests/content/test_pyproject_version_freshness.py runs, then it no longer fails with expected 0.43.0 while pyproject reports 0.44.0.

items.title: Release/version tests cover the chosen fix

narrative.Acceptance: Given the version freshness behavior is changed, when focused resolver or release tests run, then they protect the selected tag/version synchronization contract from regressing.

references.title: Issue #1564: task check: pyproject version freshness fails on master after v0.43.0

expected_outputs: version freshness and release version tests pass without masking real release/tag drift
